testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of sound mind and memory (755 ILCS 5/4-1)
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed, printed, or handwritten

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another person may sign in the testator's presence and by the testator's direction

witness_requirements.presence_rules: The testator must sign or acknowledge the will in the presence of two or more credible witnesses, who shall attest the will in the testator's presence (755 ILCS 5/4-3)
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: An interested witness does not invalidate the will. Illinois abolished the interested witness penalty

notarization.notes: Not required for validity; used for self-proving affidavit

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: Testator and witnesses sign a sworn affidavit before a notary public (755 ILCS 5/6-4)

electronic_wills.notes: Illinois enacted the Electronic Wills Act effective January 1, 2022 (755 ILCS 6/). Electronic wills must be in an electronic record, electronically signed by the testator, and witnessed by two persons. Remote witnessing is permitted via audio-video communication

holographic_wills.notes: Holographic wills are not recognized in Illinois

nuncupative_wills.notes: Oral wills are not recognized in Illinois

methods: Executing a subsequent will or codicil
Physical destruction (burning, tearing, canceling, obliterating) by the testator or at the testator's direction and in the testator's presence

special_provisions: Illinois is a separate property/common law state. Surviving spouse has an elective share of one-third of the estate if surviving descendants, or one-half if no surviving descendants (755 ILCS 5/2-8). Illinois has a state estate tax with a $4 million exemption

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of sound mind (IC 29-1-5-1)
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed, printed, or handwritten

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another person may sign in the testator's presence and at the testator's express direction

witness_requirements.presence_rules: The testator must sign or acknowledge the will in the presence of two witnesses who must sign in the testator's presence (IC 29-1-5-3)
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: An interested witness does not invalidate the will (IC 29-1-5-3)

notarization.notes: Not required for validity; used for self-proving affidavit

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: Testator and witnesses sign a sworn affidavit before a notary public (IC 29-1-5-3.1)

electronic_wills.notes: Indiana enacted electronic will provisions (IC 29-1-21). Electronic wills must be in an electronic record, electronically signed by the testator, and witnessed

holographic_wills.notes: Holographic wills are not recognized in Indiana

nuncupative_wills.notes: Recognized in limited circumstances: must be made in imminent peril of death, declared to be the will in the presence of two disinterested witnesses. Only for personal property up to $1,000 in value. Must be reduced to writing within 30 days (IC 29-1-5-4)

methods: Executing a subsequent will or codicil
Physical destruction (burning, tearing, canceling, obliterating) by the testator or at the testator's direction and in the testator's presence

special_provisions: Indiana is a separate property/common law state. Surviving spouse has an elective share: one-half of the decedent's net estate if no surviving descendants of the decedent who are not also descendants of the spouse; otherwise varies (IC 29-1-3-1). Indiana has no state estate tax

minimum_age.exceptions: Married minors may make a will (Iowa Code § 633.264)

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of full age and sound mind (Iowa Code § 633.264)
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed, printed, or handwritten

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another person may sign in the testator's presence and by the testator's express direction

witness_requirements.presence_rules: The will must be signed by the testator or by some person in the testator's presence and by the testator's express direction, and attested by two competent witnesses (Iowa Code § 633.279)
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: An interested witness does not invalidate the will

notarization.notes: Not required for validity; used for self-proving affidavit

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: Testator and witnesses sign a sworn affidavit before a notary public (Iowa Code § 633.279)

electronic_wills.notes: Iowa does not currently have an electronic wills statute

holographic_wills.notes: Holographic wills are not recognized in Iowa

nuncupative_wills.notes: Oral wills are not recognized in Iowa

methods: Executing a subsequent will or codicil
Physical destruction (burning, tearing, canceling, obliterating) by the testator or at the testator's direction

special_provisions: Iowa is a separate property/common law state. Surviving spouse has an elective share of one-third of all property that passed to others at the decedent's death (Iowa Code § 633.238). Iowa has a state inheritance tax (rates vary by relationship)
statute_citations: Iowa Code § 633.264 (Who may make a will)

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of sound mind (K.S.A. § 59-601)
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed, printed, or handwritten

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another person may sign in the testator's presence and by the testator's express direction

witness_requirements.presence_rules: The testator must sign or acknowledge the will in the presence of two or more competent witnesses who shall attest and sign the will in the testator's presence (K.S.A. § 59-606)
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: An interested witness does not invalidate the will

notarization.notes: Not required for validity; used for self-proving affidavit

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: Testator and witnesses sign a sworn affidavit before a notary public (K.S.A. § 59-606)

electronic_wills.notes: Kansas does not currently have an electronic wills statute

holographic_wills.notes: Holographic wills are not recognized in Kansas

nuncupative_wills.notes: Recognized in limited circumstances: must be made during last sickness or in imminent peril of death, before two disinterested witnesses. For personal property only. Must be reduced to writing within 30 days and proved within 6 months (K.S.A. § 59-608)

methods: Executing a subsequent will or codicil
Physical destruction (burning, tearing, canceling, obliterating) by the testator or at the testator's direction and in the testator's presence

special_provisions: Kansas is a separate property/common law state. Surviving spouse has an elective share: may elect to take one-half of the augmented estate (K.S.A. § 59-6a202). Kansas has no state estate or inheritance tax

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of sound mind (MCL 700.2501)
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed, printed, or handwritten

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another individual may sign in the testator's conscious presence and by the testator's direction (MCL 700.2502)

witness_requirements.presence_rules: Each witness must sign within a reasonable time after witnessing the signing or the testator's acknowledgment of the signature or the will
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: An interested witness does not invalidate the will (MCL 700.2505)

notarization.notes: Not required for validity; used for self-proving affidavit

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: Testator and witnesses sign a sworn affidavit before a notary public (MCL 700.2504)

electronic_wills.notes: Michigan does not currently have an electronic wills statute

holographic_wills.notes: Valid if the signature and material portions are in the testator's handwriting. Date is not required but recommended. Intent may be established from extrinsic evidence (MCL 700.2502(2))

nuncupative_wills.notes: Oral wills are not recognized in Michigan

methods: Executing a subsequent will that revokes the prior will expressly or by inconsistency
Performing a revocatory act (burning, tearing, canceling, obliterating, or destroying) by the testator or by another in the testator's conscious presence and at their direction

special_provisions: Michigan is a separate property/common law state. Surviving spouse has an elective share under the augmented estate system (MCL 700.2202). Michigan adopted the Uniform Probate Code. Michigan has no state estate tax

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of sound mind (Minn. Stat. § 524.2-501)
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed, printed, or handwritten

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another individual may sign in the testator's conscious presence and by the testator's direction (Minn. Stat. § 524.2-502)

witness_requirements.presence_rules: Each witness must sign within a reasonable time after witnessing the signing or the testator's acknowledgment of the signature or the will
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: An interested witness does not invalidate the will (Minn. Stat. § 524.2-505)

notarization.notes: Not required for validity; used for self-proving affidavit. A notarized will can serve as a self-proving will

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: Testator and witnesses sign a sworn affidavit before a notary public. Alternatively, a will may be made self-proving by acknowledgment before a notary (Minn. Stat. § 524.2-504)

electronic_wills.notes: Minnesota enacted electronic will provisions effective August 1, 2023. Electronic wills must be in an electronic record, electronically signed, and witnessed. Remote witnessing is permitted via audio-video communication

holographic_wills.notes: Holographic wills are not recognized in Minnesota

nuncupative_wills.notes: Oral wills are not recognized in Minnesota

methods: Executing a subsequent will that revokes the prior will expressly or by inconsistency
Performing a revocatory act by the testator or by another in the testator's conscious presence and at their direction

special_provisions: Minnesota is a separate property/common law state. Surviving spouse has an elective share under the augmented estate system (Minn. Stat. § 524.2-202). Minnesota adopted the Uniform Probate Code. Minnesota has a state estate tax with a $3 million exemption
statute_citations: Minn. Stat. § 524.2-501 (Who may make a will)

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of sound mind and over 18 years of age (Mo. Rev. Stat. § 474.310)
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed, printed, or handwritten

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another person may sign in the testator's presence and by the testator's express direction (Mo. Rev. Stat. § 474.320)

witness_requirements.qualifications: Must be competent witnesses at least 18 years old
witness_requirements.presence_rules: The testator must sign or acknowledge the will in the presence of two or more competent witnesses who shall attest and sign in the testator's presence (Mo. Rev. Stat. § 474.320)
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: An interested witness does not invalidate the will but the bequest may be limited to the intestate share

notarization.notes: Not required for validity; used for self-proving affidavit

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: Testator and witnesses sign a sworn affidavit before a notary public (Mo. Rev. Stat. § 474.337)

electronic_wills.notes: Missouri enacted electronic will provisions effective August 28, 2025. Electronic wills must be in an electronic record, electronically signed by the testator, and witnessed

holographic_wills.notes: Holographic wills are not recognized in Missouri

nuncupative_wills.notes: Recognized in limited circumstances: must be made during last sickness or in imminent peril of death, before two competent witnesses. For personal property only, not exceeding $500. Must be reduced to writing within 30 days and proved within 6 months (Mo. Rev. Stat. § 474.340)

methods: Executing a subsequent will or codicil
Physical destruction (burning, tearing, canceling, obliterating) by the testator or at the testator's direction and in the testator's presence

special_provisions: Missouri is a separate property/common law state. Surviving spouse has an elective share: one-third of the estate if surviving descendants, one-half if no surviving descendants (Mo. Rev. Stat. § 474.160). Missouri has no state estate or inheritance tax
statute_citations: Mo. Rev. Stat. § 474.310 (Who may make a will)

minimum_age.exceptions: Nebraska's age of majority is 19, but the will statute specifically allows those 18 or older to make a will (Neb. Rev. Stat. § 30-2326)

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of sound mind (Neb. Rev. Stat. § 30-2326)
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed, printed, or handwritten

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another individual may sign in the testator's conscious presence and by the testator's direction (Neb. Rev. Stat. § 30-2327)

witness_requirements.presence_rules: Each witness must sign within a reasonable time after witnessing the signing or the testator's acknowledgment of the signature or the will
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: An interested witness does not invalidate the will (Neb. Rev. Stat. § 30-2330)

notarization.notes: Not required for validity; used for self-proving affidavit

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: Testator and witnesses sign a sworn affidavit before a notary public (Neb. Rev. Stat. § 30-2329)

electronic_wills.notes: Nebraska does not currently have an electronic wills statute

holographic_wills.notes: Valid whether or not witnessed if the signature and material portions are in the testator's handwriting (Neb. Rev. Stat. § 30-2328)

nuncupative_wills.notes: Oral wills are not recognized in Nebraska

methods: Executing a subsequent will that revokes the prior will expressly or by inconsistency
Performing a revocatory act by the testator or by another in the testator's conscious presence and at their direction

special_provisions: Nebraska is a separate property/common law state. Surviving spouse has an elective share under the augmented estate system (Neb. Rev. Stat. § 30-2313). Nebraska adopted the Uniform Probate Code. Nebraska's age of majority is 19, but the will statute specifically allows those 18+ to make a will
statute_citations: Neb. Rev. Stat. § 30-2326 (Who may make a will)

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of sound mind (NDCC § 30.1-08-01)
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed, printed, or handwritten

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another individual may sign in the testator's conscious presence and by the testator's direction (NDCC § 30.1-08-02)

witness_requirements.presence_rules: Each witness must sign within a reasonable time after witnessing the signing or the testator's acknowledgment
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: An interested witness does not invalidate the will (NDCC § 30.1-08-05)

notarization.notes: Not required for validity; used for self-proving affidavit. A notarized will may also be self-proving

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: Testator and witnesses sign a sworn affidavit before a notary public (NDCC § 30.1-08-04)

electronic_wills.notes: North Dakota adopted the Uniform Electronic Wills Act (NDCC Ch. 30.1-37). Electronic wills must be in an electronic record, electronically signed, and witnessed

holographic_wills.notes: Valid whether or not witnessed if the signature and material portions are in the testator's handwriting (NDCC § 30.1-08-02(2))

nuncupative_wills.notes: Oral wills are not recognized in North Dakota

methods: Executing a subsequent will that revokes the prior will expressly or by inconsistency
Performing a revocatory act by the testator or by another in the testator's conscious presence and at their direction

special_provisions: North Dakota is a separate property/common law state. Surviving spouse has an elective share under the augmented estate system (NDCC § 30.1-05-02). North Dakota adopted the Uniform Probate Code. North Dakota has no state estate tax

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of sound mind and memory and not under restraint (ORC § 2107.02)
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed or printed. Ohio does not recognize holographic wills

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another person may sign in the testator's conscious presence and at the testator's express direction. The signature must be at the end of the will (ORC § 2107.03)

witness_requirements.presence_rules: The will must be attested and subscribed by two or more competent witnesses in the presence of the testator (ORC § 2107.03)
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: An interested witness may serve but the bequest to that witness is void unless there are two other disinterested witnesses (ORC § 2107.15)

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: Ohio does not have a self-proving affidavit statute. Witnesses must testify at probate or provide a deposition/affidavit

electronic_wills.notes: Ohio does not currently have an electronic wills statute

holographic_wills.notes: Holographic wills are not recognized in Ohio

nuncupative_wills.notes: Recognized in limited circumstances: must be made during last sickness, at the testator's habitual dwelling (or where they became sick), before two disinterested witnesses. Only for personal property. Must be reduced to writing within 10 days and proved within 6 months (ORC § 2107.60)

methods: Executing a subsequent will or codicil
Physical destruction (burning, tearing, canceling, obliterating) by the testator or at the testator's direction

special_provisions: Ohio is a separate property/common law state. Surviving spouse has an elective share: if one or two children or their representatives survive, one-third; if no children or their representatives survive, one-half (ORC § 2106.01). Ohio is one of the few states without a self-proving affidavit statute. Ohio requires the testator's signature at the end of the will. Ohio has a state estate tax for deaths before January 1, 2013 only

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of sound mind and over 18 years of age (84 Okla. Stat. § 41)
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed, printed, or handwritten

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another person may subscribe the testator's name in the testator's presence and by the testator's direction

witness_requirements.presence_rules: The will must be subscribed in the presence of the testator by two or more competent witnesses who saw the testator subscribe or heard the testator acknowledge the will (84 Okla. Stat. § 55)
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: A bequest to a subscribing witness is void unless there are two other disinterested subscribing witnesses

notarization.notes: Not required for validity; used for self-proving affidavit

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: Testator and witnesses sign a sworn affidavit before a notary public (58 Okla. Stat. § 71)

electronic_wills.notes: Oklahoma does not currently have an electronic wills statute

holographic_wills.notes: Valid if entirely in the testator's own handwriting and signed by the testator. No witnesses required (84 Okla. Stat. § 54)

nuncupative_wills.notes: Recognized in limited circumstances: must be made in the testator's last sickness or in imminent peril of death. Must be proved by two witnesses. For personal property only up to $1,000 (84 Okla. Stat. § 46)

methods: Executing a subsequent will or codicil
Physical destruction (burning, tearing, canceling, obliterating) by the testator or at the testator's direction and in the testator's presence

special_provisions: Oklahoma is a separate property/common law state. Surviving spouse has an elective share: forced share of one-half of property acquired during the marriage by joint industry (84 Okla. Stat. § 44). Oklahoma has no state estate or inheritance tax
statute_citations: 84 Okla. Stat. § 41 (Who may make a will)

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of sound mind (SDCL § 29A-2-501)
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed, printed, or handwritten

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another individual may sign in the testator's conscious presence and by the testator's direction (SDCL § 29A-2-502)

witness_requirements.presence_rules: Each witness must sign within a reasonable time after witnessing the signing or the testator's acknowledgment
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: An interested witness does not invalidate the will (SDCL § 29A-2-505)

notarization.notes: Not required for validity; used for self-proving affidavit

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: Testator and witnesses sign a sworn affidavit before a notary public (SDCL § 29A-2-504)

electronic_wills.notes: South Dakota does not currently have an electronic wills statute

holographic_wills.notes: Valid whether or not witnessed if the signature and material portions are in the testator's handwriting (SDCL § 29A-2-502(2))

nuncupative_wills.notes: Oral wills are not recognized in South Dakota

methods: Executing a subsequent will that revokes the prior will expressly or by inconsistency
Performing a revocatory act by the testator or by another in the testator's conscious presence and at their direction

special_provisions: South Dakota is a separate property/common law state. Surviving spouse has an elective share under the augmented estate system (SDCL § 29A-2-202). South Dakota adopted the Uniform Probate Code. South Dakota has no state income tax, estate tax, or inheritance tax. South Dakota is known for its favorable trust laws

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of sound mind (Wis. Stat. § 853.01)
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed, printed, or handwritten

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another individual may sign in the testator's conscious presence and by the testator's direction (Wis. Stat. § 853.03)

witness_requirements.presence_rules: Each witness must sign within a reasonable time after witnessing the signing or the testator's acknowledgment. Wisconsin enacted remote witnessing via attorney-supervised audiovisual communication in 2024 (2023 Act 130)
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: An interested witness does not invalidate the will (Wis. Stat. § 853.07)

notarization.notes: Not required for validity; used for self-proving affidavit

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: Testator and witnesses sign a sworn affidavit before a notary public (Wis. Stat. § 853.04)

electronic_wills.notes: Wisconsin does not have a full electronic wills statute but enacted remote witnessing provisions via attorney-supervised audiovisual communication in 2024 (2023 Act 130)

holographic_wills.notes: Holographic wills are not recognized in Wisconsin

nuncupative_wills.notes: Oral wills are not recognized in Wisconsin

methods: Executing a subsequent will that revokes the prior will expressly or by inconsistency
Performing a revocatory act by the testator or by another in the testator's conscious presence and at their direction

special_provisions: Wisconsin is a COMMUNITY PROPERTY state — the only one in the Midwest. It adopted the Uniform Marital Property Act (Wis. Stat. Ch. 766). Surviving spouse has an elective share of the augmented deferred marital property estate (Wis. Stat. § 861.02). Wisconsin enacted remote audiovisual witnessing supervised by an attorney in 2024 (2023 Act 130)
statute_citations: Wis. Stat. § 853.01 (Who may make a will)
